title: Create BST from given sorted array

content.problemStatement:
Create a height-balanced binary search tree from the given sorted integer array.

(**Note:** A height-balanced binary tree is defined as a binary tree in which the depth difference between the two subtrees of every node is never more than one.)

For eg., the BST that can be created from the sorted array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7] is as follows:




Input Format

The first line of the input contains s single integer NNN — the number of integers in the sorted array.
Next line contains NNN space separated sorted integers of the array.


Output Format
Complete the given function which returns the root of created BST.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the sorted array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers in non‑decreasing order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the text: Successfully created BST!

hidden.label: stress case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Successfully created BST!
Successfully created BST!
Successfully created BST!

hidden.label: stress case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Successfully created BST!
Successfully created BST!

hidden.label: stress case 3
hidden.expectedOutput:
Successfully created BST!
Successfully created BST!
Successfully created BST!
Successfully created BST!

solution.approach: The array is already sorted, so the middle element can be chosen as the root to guarantee height‑balance. Recursively apply the same rule to the left sub‑array to build the left subtree and to the right sub‑array for the right subtree. This divide‑and‑conquer approach constructs a balanced BST in linear time. The recursion depth is at most O(log N) for a balanced tree, which fits comfortably in the call stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (each element is visited once)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) total – the tree nodes plus O(log N) recursion stack
